common.notFoundBody: The page you were looking for doesn't exist or has moved.
common.somethingWrongBody: This page could not load. The problem has been logged with our team.

hero.title: Replay any decision.
hero.description: When a regulator or customer asks how a decision was made, pull the exact rules and data from that moment and recompute the identical result — not a log entry, the real answer. Two independent engines prove the execution byte-for-byte, a hash-chained audit makes it tamper-evident, and AI drafts while humans approve. For loan approvals, KYC exceptions, claims, and access policies.
hero.builtOnAsterLang: Built on the open-source Aster Lang engine. Read the language docs →

compiled.title: Compiled, not interpreted
compiled.description: Truffle on GraalVM runs your rules at JVM speed. Same .class output across machines.

audited.title: Audit-trail by default
audited.description: Every decision pinned to a SHA-256 hash chain. Replay any historical evaluation byte-for-byte.

multilang.title: Three lexicons today
multilang.description: English, 中文, Deutsch ship in every plan (EN canonical; ZH/DE community-maintained). Custom industry lexicons on Enterprise.

nativeLanguage.title: Native-language CNL
nativeLanguage.description: Write rules in English, 中文, or Deutsch — same semantics across all three lexicon packs.

aiDraftHumanReview.title: AI drafts, humans approve
aiDraftHumanReview.description: Let AI generate the first cut. Reviewers, not authors, approve every published version.

hashChainAudit.title: Tamper-evident audit
hashChainAudit.description: SHA-256 hash chain over every decision. Replay any historical evaluation byte-for-byte.

dualEngineSemantics.title: Java + TypeScript dual engine
dualEngineSemantics.description: Identical results from the Truffle backend and the in-browser TypeScript engine. No semantic drift.

multiLanguagePacks.title: Multi-language lexicon packs
multiLanguagePacks.description: English, 中文, Deutsch ship in every plan. Custom industry lexicons available on Enterprise.

selfHostable.title: Self-host on your cluster
selfHostable.description: Helm chart, K3S, ArgoCD GitOps. Your data stays inside your perimeter.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free, upgrade when you need more

cta.title: Run your credit rules through Aster — free
cta.description: Bring a real loan-approval or limit rule. We'll show you the full chain: approve the rule, pin the version, execute, and replay any decision for an auditor. No credit card.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing and using Aster Cloud, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement.

services.title: 2. Description of Services
services.content: Aster Cloud provides a policy management platform that allows users to create, test, and execute business rules. Our services include policy creation and management, PII detection, compliance reporting, and API access.

accounts.title: 3. User Accounts
accounts.content: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized use of your account.

usage.title: 4. Acceptable Use
usage.content: You agree not to use our services for any unlawful purpose or in any way that could damage, disable, or impair our services. You may not attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the service.

payment.title: 5. Payment Terms
payment.content: Paid subscriptions are billed in advance on a monthly or annual basis. All fees are non-refundable except as expressly set forth in our refund policy.

termination.title: 6. Termination
termination.content: We may terminate or suspend your account at any time for violations of these terms. You may cancel your subscription at any time through your account settings.

liability.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
liability.content: Aster Cloud shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of our services.

changes.title: 8. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. We will notify users of any material changes via email or through the service.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: Aster offers two deployment modes: a multi-tenant SaaS at aster-lang.cloud, and a self-hosted on-prem build that you operate yourself. This policy covers both. The on-prem build never sends data to Aster except for opt-in usage telemetry described below — your policy content, decision traces, and end-user data stay on infrastructure you control.

collection.title: 1. Information we collect
collection.content: SaaS account: name, email, billing address, payment method (Stripe-tokenized; we never see raw card data). Usage: workspace, policy, and API call metadata required to run the service. On-prem deployments only send what you explicitly opt into; see the Telemetry section below.

usage.title: 2. How we use your information
usage.content: Operating the service (provisioning, billing, authentication), preventing abuse (rate limiting, fraud detection), customer support, legal compliance, and capacity planning. We do not sell personal data and do not use customer data to train AI models.

sharing.title: 3. Information sharing
sharing.content: We share with sub-processors (Stripe for billing, Resend for transactional email, Cloudflare for edge protection, our cloud provider for hosting). Each is bound by a written agreement and processes data only on our instructions. See the Sub-processors section for the current list.

telemetry.title: 4. On-prem usage telemetry (opt-in)
telemetry.content: If you opt in with ASTER_TELEMETRY_OPT_IN=1, your on-prem deployment sends a weekly batch of aggregate counters (active seats, policy execution counts, features used) to our SaaS ingest endpoint. Every field is an integer or boolean; no user identifiers, no policy content, no decision traces. The full machine-readable contract is at /api/v1/telemetry/schema and the per-field necessity rationale is documented in docs/on-prem/telemetry-fields.md.

transfers.title: 5. International data transfers
transfers.content: Aster SaaS currently operates from a single region. Each telemetry row is tagged with the SaaS region that accepted it (us / eu / apac / unknown), exposed in the admin transparency panel and in DSAR access responses. For customers in restricted jurisdictions we offer Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) via our DPA template.

subprocessors.title: 6. Sub-processors
subprocessors.content: Stripe (payment processing, US), Resend (transactional email, US), Cloudflare (edge protection, global), Oracle Cloud (hosting, US/EU regions as contracted). New sub-processors are announced via the changelog at least 30 days before they begin processing data; existing customers may object before that window closes.

security.title: 7. Data security
security.content: TLS 1.3 in transit. Database encrypted at rest with provider-managed AES-256. Telemetry HMAC verification keys are additionally envelope-encrypted (AES-256-GCM) under a key held in our secrets manager — a database compromise alone does not yield usable keys. Access to production data is logged for SOC 2 evidence; every read of telemetry rows generates an audit record.

retention.title: 8. Data retention
retention.content: SaaS account data is retained while the account is active and for a brief wind-down after cancellation. On-prem telemetry rows are deleted automatically 365 days after receipt by a nightly retention job. Access-audit records have a 90-day window; deletion-audit records have a 7-year hold for regulatory evidence. Configurable via TELEMETRY_RETENTION_DAYS.

rights.title: 9. Your rights
rights.content: Subject to GDPR / CCPA: rights of access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction, and objection. For SaaS account data, contact privacy@aster-lang.cloud. For on-prem telemetry, use the self-service DSAR API below — no Aster support ticket required.

dsar.title: 10. Self-service DSAR for telemetry
dsar.content: POST /api/v1/dsar with HMAC-signed body to request access (action='access') or deletion (action='delete'). dryRun=true previews the affected row count without committing. Both actions are recorded in the immutable audit log so we can prove fulfillment to regulators. Full curl examples and the request schema are in docs/on-prem/dsar.md.

cookies.title: 11. Cookies and tracking
cookies.content: We use first-party cookies for session continuity (auth, preferences). We do not use third-party advertising cookies. Mixpanel product-analytics is loaded on SaaS pages with anonymized identifiers only; you can disable it via your account preferences.

contact.title: 12. Contact us
contact.content: Privacy questions: privacy@aster-lang.cloud. Data Protection Officer: dpo@aster-lang.cloud. EU representative: see the DPA template in docs/on-prem/dpa-template.md. Supervisory-authority complaints may be filed in the jurisdiction where you reside.

whatIsAster.question: What is Aster Cloud?
whatIsAster.answer: Aster Cloud is a policy management platform that lets you define, test, and execute business rules as code. It's ideal for compliance automation, decision engines, and business logic that changes frequently without code deployments.

whatIsPolicy.question: What is a policy and how do I create one?
whatIsPolicy.answer: A policy is a set of business rules written in Aster's human-readable language. For example: 'if credit_score >= 700 and income > 50000 then approve_loan'. Create policies from the dashboard using our visual editor or by writing rules directly.

piiDetection.question: How does PII detection work?
piiDetection.answer: Our AI automatically scans your policies for personally identifiable information (PII) like email addresses, phone numbers, SSN, and financial data. Detected PII is flagged in reports to help you maintain compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, and other regulations.

complianceStandards.question: What compliance standards do you support?
complianceStandards.answer: We generate compliance reports for GDPR (data protection), HIPAA (healthcare), SOC 2 (security controls), and PCI-DSS (payment data). Reports analyze your policies and provide actionable recommendations.

integration.question: Can I integrate Aster Cloud with my applications?
integration.answer: Yes! Use our REST API to execute policies from any application. We provide code examples for JavaScript, Python, cURL, and more. API keys can be managed from Settings > API Keys.

selfHosted.question: Is there a self-hosted option?
selfHosted.answer: Enterprise customers can request on-premise deployment with dedicated support, custom SLA guarantees, and private cloud options. Contact our sales team for details.

downgrade.question: What happens to my policies when I downgrade?
downgrade.answer: When you downgrade to a lower plan, policies beyond your new limit are automatically frozen (newest policies remain active). Frozen policies can still be viewed but cannot be executed or edited. To unfreeze, either delete some policies or upgrade your plan. Your policy data is never deleted during a downgrade.

cancelAnytime.question: Can I cancel my subscription anytime?
cancelAnytime.answer: Yes, you can cancel your subscription at any time. Your access will continue until the end of your current billing period.

trialEnds.question: What happens when my trial ends?
trialEnds.answer: After your trial ends, you'll be automatically downgraded to the Free plan. You can upgrade to Pro or Team at any time to regain access to premium features.

refunds.question: Do you offer refunds?
refunds.answer: We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. If you're not satisfied with your subscription, contact us within 30 days for a full refund.

apiVsExecutions.question: What's the difference between API calls and executions?
apiVsExecutions.answer: Executions count all policy runs (web UI + API). API calls only count requests made via the REST API. Each API request consumes both quotas. Web UI usage only consumes executions.

upgradePlan.question: How do I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
upgradePlan.answer: You can upgrade anytime from this page. Your new plan takes effect immediately. To downgrade, cancel your current subscription and it will revert to Free at the end of the billing period.

dataSecurity.question: How is my data secured?
dataSecurity.answer: All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256). We follow SOC 2 Type II practices and never share your policy data with third parties.

invoices.question: Can I get invoices for my subscription?
invoices.answer: Yes, invoices are automatically generated and sent to your email after each payment. You can also download past invoices from your Stripe customer portal.

freeLimits.question: What are the limitations of the Free plan?
freeLimits.answer: Free plan includes 3 saved policies, 100 executions/month, and basic PII detection. API access and compliance reports require a Pro or Team plan.

support.question: How can I contact support?
support.answer: Free users can reach us via email at support(at)aster-lang.cloud. Pro users get priority support with faster response times.

versions.title: Versions & approvals

submitDialog.title: Submit version {n} for approval?
submitDialog.body: This locks the source — further edits go into a new draft version. Approvers will be notified.

approveDialog.title: Approve version {n}?
approveDialog.body: Approved versions are eligible to execute. You can mark it as default after approval.

rejectDialog.title: Reject version {n}?
rejectDialog.body: The author will see your comment and can address it in a new draft.

setDefaultDialog.title: Make version {n} the default?
setDefaultDialog.body: New executions without an explicit version pin will use v{n}. Existing pinned executions continue to use their pinned version.

deprecateDialog.title: Deprecate version {n}?
deprecateDialog.body: Deprecated versions can still execute when pinned, but the default will move off this version.

archiveDialog.title: Archive version {n}?
archiveDialog.body: Archived versions cannot execute and are hidden from default execution. This is permanent — undo requires creating a new version.

sox.selfApprovalNotAllowed: You cannot approve a version you authored. Ask another team member.
sox.inviteReviewerRequired: Approval requires a second reviewer on your plan. Invite a team member to continue.

examples.getPolicyId: How to find a policy module + function
examples.getPolicyIdDesc: Open any policy in the Policies page; the module name (e.g. aster.finance.loan) and the entry function name (e.g. evaluateLoanEligibility) are shown in the editor header. You will pass both to /api/v1/policies/evaluate.

examples.listPoliciesDesc: Returns every version of a given policy together with its publish status — useful when auditing a decision.
examples.responseExampleDesc: Successful evaluation returns { result, executionTimeMs, error }. result is the policy's return value; error is null on success.
examples.errorHandlingDesc: Common HTTP status codes you may encounter when using the API.
examples.error429: Rate limit exceeded, please retry later

dataPage.title: Your AI data
dataPage.subtitle: Under GDPR Articles 15 and 17 you have the right to access and erase the AI conversations we store. Use the actions below.

downloadCard.title: Download a copy
downloadCard.body: Exports every AI call we have for you (prompt + completion + safety flags) as a JSON file. Useful for compliance archives or to migrate elsewhere.

deleteCard.title: Erase AI conversation content
deleteCard.body: Permanently clears the encrypted prompt + completion + redacted text of every AI call you have made. We keep aggregate counters (tokens, cost, status) for billing and abuse detection — those are GDPR-permitted.
deleteCard.confirmBody: This wipes the original prompts and completions across {count, plural, =0 {no records} one {# record} other {# records}}. Counts and billing metadata are kept.
deleteCard.success: {count, plural, =0 {Nothing to erase} one {# record erased} other {# records erased}}

aiCircuitBreakerPage.title: AI circuit breaker
aiCircuitBreakerPage.subtitle: Manual override for the platform-wide LLM kill switch. Use only when an upstream LLM provider is sustained-degraded and we need to stop new calls from accruing cost.
aiCircuitBreakerPage.releaseConfirmBody: New AI calls from all plans will be allowed again. Only do this if you've confirmed the upstream incident is resolved.

aiKeysPage.title: AI Keys (Bring Your Own Key)
aiKeysPage.subtitle: Bind your own OpenAI / Anthropic / Vertex AI key. AI calls use your quota and bypass the platform LLM monthly limit.
aiKeysPage.apiKeyHint: Stored encrypted via pgcrypto; the plaintext is unrecoverable.
aiKeysPage.keyTooShort: API key is too short — at least 20 characters required.
aiKeysPage.noneBound: No BYOK keys bound. AI calls will use the platform monthly quota.
aiKeysPage.whyQuota: Doesn't consume the platform AI monthly quota (Free 20 / Pro 500 / Team 500 per seat).
aiKeysPage.whyBilling: Calls bill directly to your provider account using your rate limits.
aiKeysPage.whyCompliance: Data stays out of third-parties in compliance scenarios (Vertex runs in your GCP project).
aiKeysPage.revokeDialogBody: This permanently deletes your {provider} key. AI calls will fall back to the platform quota. This cannot be undone.
aiKeysPage.providerUrlHint: Custom API base URL (e.g. an OpenAI-compatible gateway). Leave blank for the default OpenAI/Anthropic endpoint. Custom domains must be approved by an administrator; unapproved URLs fall back with an error.
aiKeysPage.tokenQuotaHint: Cap the tokens this key may spend per month. Leave blank for unlimited.
aiKeysPage.expiresAtHint: The key stops being used after this date. Leave blank for no expiry.
aiKeysPage.quotaInvalid: Token quota must be a positive whole number.

license.title: License
license.subtitle: Enterprise license status, signature trust, and revocation freshness for this on-prem deployment.

missing.title: License key not configured
missing.body: Set the LICENSE_KEY environment variable. The deployment will remain in limited mode until a valid license is available.

malformed.title: License key is malformed
malformed.body: The configured license cannot be parsed. Contact sales for a replacement license key.

signatureInvalid.title: License signature is invalid
signatureInvalid.body: The license payload does not match its signature. Treat this as a tampered key or a key signed for another payload.

signatureUntrustedKey.title: License signing key is not trusted
signatureUntrustedKey.body: The license was signed by a key that is not in this build's trust bundle. Contact sales for a reissued key.

bindingMismatch.title: License is bound to a different deployment
bindingMismatch.body: This license signature is valid, but its deploymentBinding does not match the ASTER_DEPLOYMENT_ID configured on this instance. Either you are running the license on the wrong cluster, or the ASTER_DEPLOYMENT_ID env var is missing or misconfigured. Contact sales for a reissued license bound to this deployment.

legacyUnsigned.title: Unsigned legacy license
legacyUnsigned.body: This v1 license is accepted only during the migration window. Upgrade to a signed v2 license before the deadline.
legacyUnsigned.deadline: Upgrade to v2 signing before the compatibility window closes.

verifiedRevoked.title: License revoked
verifiedRevoked.body: This license is cryptographically valid but appears on the revocation list. Contact sales or support.

verifiedExpired.title: License expired
verifiedExpired.body: {days, plural, one {Expired # day ago} other {Expired # days ago}}

networkGraceExpired.title: Revocation grace period ended
networkGraceExpired.body: The license signature is valid, but this deployment has not reached the revocation endpoint within the allowed grace period.

verifiedExpiringSoon.title: License expires soon
verifiedExpiringSoon.body: {days, plural, one {# day remaining} other {# days remaining}}

networkGrace.title: Revocation check temporarily stale
networkGrace.body: Temporary connectivity issue. Last successful check was {minutes, plural, one {# minute ago} other {# minutes ago}}.

verifiedActive.title: License verified active
verifiedActive.body: Signature, entitlement, and revocation freshness are valid.

advisory.heading: Advisories
advisory.expiringSoon: License renewal is approaching: {days, plural, one {# day remaining} other {# days remaining}}.
advisory.revocationStale: The latest revocation fetch failed; the last known-good result is still being used.
advisory.networkGrace: Revocation status is in grace. Last successful check was {minutes, plural, one {# minute ago} other {# minutes ago}}.
advisory.legacyUnsignedActive: This unsigned v1 license should be replaced with a signed v2 license.

revocation.heading: Revocation Status
revocation.grace: Temporary connectivity issue — grace period active
revocation.graceExpired: Connectivity restored required — grace period ended

oidc.appHint: Configure these values in your OpenID Connect provider when registering Aster as a relying party.
oidc.idpHeading: Identity Provider — values Aster reads from your env
oidc.idpHint: Provide the issuer URL, client ID, and client secret. Scopes default to openid email profile.

reason.clock-rollback: The system clock appears to have moved backwards beyond the safety tolerance. Restore correct time via NTP — license verification has been suspended to prevent expired-license replay.

published_rules.title: Free plan is limited to {limit} published rules

evaluations.title: Monthly evaluation limit reached
evaluations.body: Free includes {limit} evaluations / month. Pro raises this to 50,000, with overage billing.

audit_retention.title: Free retains audit logs for 7 days
audit_retention.body: Enterprise gives you unlimited retention with hash-chained audit and digital signatures, suitable for SOX / HIPAA / PCI.

sso.title: SSO is an Enterprise feature

data_residency.title: Data residency requires Enterprise
data_residency.body: Choose your jurisdiction (EU / US / China / custom) on the Enterprise plan via VPC or self-hosted K3S deployment.

reviewer_required.title: Approval requires a 2-person team
reviewer_required.body: Pro enables SOX Segregation of Duties: the author and reviewer must be different. Upgrade to Pro to invite a reviewer.

team_member_invite.title: Free is single-seat
team_member_invite.body: Pro lets you invite teammates with role-based access. Upgrade to add a member.

seo.title: Replay a credit decision — Aster Lang demo
seo.description: See it end to end: a real loan-approval rule, a sample applicant, the decision, and a byte-for-byte replay you'd show a regulator. No signup.

demoPage.title: Replay a credit decision
demoPage.subtitle: Pick an applicant, see the decision, then replay exactly how it was reached — the answer you give a regulator, not a guess.

step1.title: The credit rule
step1.hint: Written in plain, controlled English — readable by risk and compliance, not just engineers. This is the rule that decides every application.

step2.title: Pick an applicant
step2.hint: Three real-world cases: a clean approval, a borderline referral, and a decline.

step3.title: Tune the thresholds
step3.hint: These cut-offs ARE the rule — edit them and the rule above rewrites itself. This is the policy change a credit officer would actually make.

step4.title: Replay this decision
step4.auditorNote: This is what you hand an auditor. Every step that led to the decision, recomputed from the exact rule and inputs — not a log entry, the real path.

adverse.label: Adverse-action reason

reasons.creditScore: Credit score of {actual} is below the {threshold} minimum required for approval.
reasons.tierMiss: Credit score of {actual} did not meet the {threshold} threshold for automatic approval; sent to manual review.
reasons.overCap: Requested amount of {actual} exceeds the affordability cap of {threshold} (income-based); sent to manual review for an oversized loan.

adverse.note: This is the disclosable reason required for adverse credit decisions — and the replay below is its step-by-step proof, recomputed from the exact rule and inputs.

cta.title: Bring your own credit rules
cta.subtitle: Run a real loan-approval or limit rule through Aster and replay any decision. Free to start.

demoPage.runHint: Edit any input or threshold above, then run — the decision is computed live by the same engine that runs in production.

explanation.heading: Why this decision
explanation.sub: Computed directly from the rule and the inputs — every value is exact, not generated.
explanation.aiNarrativeHint: Optional plain-language read of the facts above.

seo.title: Dual-Engine Parity — Aster Lang
seo.description: Track how Aster Lang's Java and TypeScript engines agree, layer by layer. Parse parity (both engines accept the same source) and runtime eval parity (both produce identical output) are both measured nightly and published openly, with a ledger of resolved divergences.

hero.title: Dual-Engine Parity
hero.subtitle: Aster Lang ships two production engines (Java and TypeScript). We test them against a shared corpus every night and publish layered evidence — parse acceptance and runtime output equality — plus an open ledger of the divergences we've already closed.

equivalencePage.unavailable: Live data unavailable right now. See the repository for the latest measurement.
equivalencePage.measuredOn: {equivalent} / {total} Tier 1 samples accepted by both engines — measured {date}
equivalencePage.qualifier: Parse-level: this measures whether both engines accept the same source. Runtime output equality is measured separately as eval parity (below). Both layers are tracked openly.
equivalencePage.progress: Parse parity improved from {initial} on {date} by +{delta} percentage points across {count} measurements, under the current Tier 1 parse denominator (tier2 divergent samples are tracked separately, not in this denominator).

evalParity.heading: Runtime eval parity
evalParity.intro: The stronger metric: both engines evaluate the same inputs and must produce identical output (not just accept the source). Measured nightly over the golden cases.
evalParity.measuredOn: {identical} / {total} golden cases produce identical output across both engines — measured {date}

resolvedDivergences.heading: Resolved runtime divergences
resolvedDivergences.intro: Cases both engines once accepted but evaluated differently — now fixed and locked by golden tests. We publish the closed ledger because tracking divergences to resolution is the trust signal, not hiding that they existed.

rows.resolution: int/double numeric promotion; string-concat keeps both operands
/ is float division on both engines (byte-identical)
added real modulo operator; 09 restored to a genuine even-check

footer.heading: What this measures (and what it doesn't)
items: Source corpus: aster-cloud/aster-lang-test, Tier 1 parity samples only (tier2 divergent and tier3 single-engine fixtures are excluded from these denominators).
Parse parity: a sample passes only if both engines parse/accept it. Eval parity: both engines must produce identical runtime output on the golden cases. Eval parity is the stronger guarantee.
Not yet covered by these rates: Core IR field-level parity, type-checking, and error-diagnostic parity (tracked separately).
Both rates are re-measured nightly at 02:00 UTC via the nightly-equivalence GitHub Action; history committed back to equivalence-history.csv and eval-history.csv.
See the dual-engine syntax baseline RFC and DIVERGENT-MANIFEST.md for the full methodology and the resolved-divergence ledger.

whitepaperCta.heading: The white paper
whitepaperCta.body: A plain-language explanation of dual-engine equivalence for risk & compliance — with the evidence, a worked credit example, and an honest scope. Read it online or download as PDF.

seo.title: Pricing — Aster Lang
seo.description: Policy-as-Code in plain English (and 中文 / Deutsch). Free for individuals. Pro for teams. Enterprise for regulated industries.

hero.title: Simple pricing for serious policy work
hero.subtitle: Free to try. Pay when your team needs separation of duties.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
features: Unlimited AI drafts via BYOK (your OpenAI / Anthropic / Vertex key)
Data residency: EU / US / China / custom

adminLaunchpad.title: Set Aster up in 60 seconds
adminLaunchpad.body: You’re signed in as an admin. Tick these five boxes to take the platform from empty to ready-for-production.

createPolicy.label: Create your first policy
createPolicy.desc: Write a CNL rule + decision schema. Aster compiles and runs it from the browser.

addAiKey.label: Connect an AI key (BYOK)
addAiKey.desc: Bring your own OpenAI / Anthropic / Vertex AI key so AI drafts bypass the platform monthly cap.

inviteTeam.label: Invite a teammate
inviteTeam.desc: Set up reviewers and approvers so policy changes flow through your existing process.

reviewBilling.label: Review billing & seats
reviewBilling.desc: Check trial days left, add a payment method, see what each plan unlocks.

empty.title: No terms yet

needsUpgrade.trialEndedDescription: Your trial ended on {date}. Upgrade to keep the vocabulary surface available.

downgrade.trialEndedDescription: Trial ended on {date}. Some Pro features are now read-only.

bulk.title: Bulk import vocabulary
bulk.description: Paste CSV (with a header row) or a JSON array. Aliases are pipe-separated in CSV (e.g. amount|amt) or a JSON array.
bulk.rowsValid: Sync up to {syncLimit}; async up to {asyncLimit}
bulk.parseErrorsTitle: Some rows could not be parsed
bulk.syncDescription: Apply immediately. Up to {limit} rows per request.
bulk.asyncDescription: Queue a worker. Up to {limit} rows; poll the progress card.
bulk.asyncProRequired: Async jobs require a Pro plan or higher.
bulk.errorGeneric: Could not start the import. Please retry.
bulk.errorReadFile: Could not read the selected file.

snapshotsView.title: Vocabulary snapshots

empty.title: No snapshots yet

apiKey.description: Issue an API key with at least the MEMBER role so the policy evaluation endpoints accept your requests.

viewTraces.description: After the first evaluation lands, the policies page lists every recent run and lets you drill into each decision trace.

fallbackBanner.title: Translation in progress
fallbackBanner.body: This page has not been translated yet. The English original is shown below.

seo.title: Write rules in your own industry terms — Aster Lang demo
seo.description: Healthcare, insurance, logistics: write the rule in your domain's vocabulary and the engine compiles and runs it. No signup.

vocabDemoPage.title: Your words. The same engine.
vocabDemoPage.subtitle: Write rules in your industry's own vocabulary — the engine maps your terms to its canonical form, compiles, and runs them. One language, every domain.

step1.title: Pick a domain
step1.hint: Three industries, three vocabularies — the same engine underneath.

step2.title: Your domain vocabulary
step2.hint: These are your industry words. The engine knows what each maps to internally.

step3.title: The rule, in your words
step3.hint: Written entirely in the industry vocabulary above — readable by the people who own the policy.
step3.legend: = your industry vocabulary (the engine translates these to its canonical form).

step4.title: Run a case
step4.hint: Pick a case; the browser engine injects your vocabulary, compiles, and computes the decision live.

terms.note: The industry term is what you write; the engine term is what it compiles to. Same logic, your words.

result.label: Decision
result.note: Computed live by the same engine that runs in production — your vocabulary translated to canonical form, then executed.

cta.title: Bring your own vocabulary
cta.subtitle: Define your domain's terms once; your whole team writes rules in words they already use.

seo.title: The Cat Mood Engine — a poetic Aster Lang demo
seo.description: Write a rule in cat-whisperer vocabulary, run it through the real engine, and watch a 5-second sketch cat react. Domain vocabulary, but make it whiskers.

catMoodPage.title: The Cat Mood Engine
catMoodPage.subtitle: A rule written in the sacred vocabulary of cat-whisperers — compiled and run by the very same engine that decides loans. The cat does not care, but it will react.
catMoodPage.poem:
Where the sunbeam pools and the bowl is full,
the small god decides what mood to rule.
No intruder? Then warmth, or a purr, or — pfft —
the slow blink of judgment, exquisitely cool.
catMoodPage.legend: = cat vocabulary the engine learned for this demo.

perch.name: 🐈 Perch
perch.quip: High ground secured. From this throne the whole domain is under surveillance. As it should be.

purr.name: 😌 Purr
purr.quip: Belly full, world forgiven. The motor is running. You may continue to exist.

loaf.name: 🍞 Loaf
loaf.quip: Warmth detected. Bones now optional. The cat has become bread.

floof.name: 🙀 Floof
floof.quip: A STRANGER. Double in size, triple in suspicion. This is fine. (It is not fine.)

judge.name: 😼 Judgment
judge.quip: No sun, no snacks, no patience. You are being evaluated. You did not pass.

catMoodPage.footer: Silly vocabulary, serious engine: this cat rule compiled and ran on the same Aster Lang runtime as a credit policy. Your words — even these — become an auditable decision.
catMoodPage.idleHint: The cat is doing its own thing. Pick a scene below to see how it reacts.

languageSettings.title: Language availability
languageSettings.description: Choose which languages are available to your team's users. Disabled languages will not appear in the language switcher.
languageSettings.defaultLocaleNote: The default language cannot be disabled.
languageSettings.permissionDenied: Only team owners or admins can change languages.

platformLanguageSettings.title: Platform language availability
platformLanguageSettings.description: Choose which languages are available across the entire platform — both the app and the docs site (aster-lang.dev). Disabling a language takes it offline everywhere: its UI, its lexicon, and writing policies in it. Team-level language settings are a subset of this.
platformLanguageSettings.defaultLocaleNote: Default language — always available, cannot be disabled.
platformLanguageSettings.saveFailed: Could not update. Please try again.

byokAllowlistSettings.title: BYOK endpoint allowlist
byokAllowlistSettings.description: Hosts that BYOK custom Provider URLs may point to (server-side SSRF boundary). Changes take effect immediately across all replicas — no redeploy.
byokAllowlistSettings.mutateFailed: Change failed. The host may be invalid or resolve to a private address.
